questions.explanation: In Burrow, an eager young rabbit tries to dig her dream home alone before learning to ask neighbors for help.

questions.question: In Sanjay's Super Team, a boy imagines cartoon superheroes during what real-life activity?
questions.explanation: Sanjay's Super Team shows a boy's cartoon-fueled imagination transforming his father's quiet prayer ritual into an epic battle.

questions.question: The Pixar short Out involves a young man coming out to his parents using what device?
answers: A magical power to swap bodies with his dog
questions.explanation: In the short Out, a young man gains the magical ability to swap bodies with his dog while coming out to his parents.

questions.question: What is the surname of Andy, the toys' owner in the original "Toy Story" trilogy?
questions.explanation: Andy Davis is the boy who owns Woody, Buzz, and the rest of the toy gang across the Toy Story trilogy.

questions.question: In the original Toy Story, what kind of toy is the anxious Rex?
questions.explanation: Rex is the nervous green Tyrannosaurus rex toy in Andy's room.

questions.question: In "Toy Story 2," Woody learns he was merchandise from what 1950s puppet show?
questions.explanation: Woody discovers his origins as the star of the vintage children's program Woody's Roundup.

questions.question: What is the name of Sid Phillips' younger sister in "Toy Story"?
questions.explanation: Hannah is Sid's sister whose dolls he cannibalizes for parts to make his mutant toys.

questions.question: Who is the tyrannical grasshopper leader threatening Ant Island in "A Bug's Life"?
questions.explanation: Hopper leads the grasshopper gang that extorts a yearly food tribute from the ant colony.

questions.question: In "A Bug's Life," the short-tempered circus performer Francis belongs to which insect species?
questions.explanation: Francis is a male ladybug in P.T. Flea's circus troupe who is frequently mistaken for female.

questions.question: What is the real name of the toddler nicknamed "Boo" in "Monsters, Inc."?
questions.explanation: A drawing in her bedroom reveals that Boo's actual name is Mary.

questions.question: Which Monsters, Inc. executive is revealed to be conspiring with Randall against Sulley?
questions.explanation: Company chairman Henry J. Waternoose secretly backs Randall's scheme to solve the energy crisis by force.

questions.question: What is the street address of the dentist who captures Nemo in "Finding Nemo"?
questions.explanation: Marlin repeats the address 42 Wallaby Way, Sydney throughout his journey to rescue Nemo.

questions.question: In "Finding Nemo," how old does the sea turtle Crush say he is?
questions.explanation: Crush proudly tells Marlin and Dory that he is a youthful 150 years old.

questions.question: In The Incredibles, what is the name of Syndrome's giant robot weapon?
questions.explanation: The Omnidroid is Syndrome's learning combat robot that adapts to any hero.

questions.question: In The Incredibles, which ice-powered hero is the Parr family's friend?
questions.explanation: Lucius Best, known as Frozone, is Mr. Incredible's ice-wielding best friend.

questions.question: Which "Incredibles" character designs superhero suits and refuses to include capes?
questions.explanation: Fashion designer Edna Mode outfits supers but bans capes after past client mishaps.

questions.question: Radiator Springs in "Cars" sits along which historic American highway?
questions.explanation: Radiator Springs is a fading town bypassed decades earlier by the interstate on old Route 66.

questions.question: What racing name did Doc Hudson compete under before becoming a small-town judge?
questions.explanation: Doc Hudson was once a champion racer known as the Fabulous Hudson Hornet.

questions.question: Who is Lightning McQueen's chief on-track rival for the Piston Cup in "Cars"?
questions.explanation: Chick Hicks is the arrogant veteran racer determined to beat Lightning McQueen to the Piston Cup.

questions.question: What is the name of Remy's father, who leads his rat clan, in "Ratatouille"?
questions.explanation: Django leads Remy's rat colony and struggles to accept his son's dream of cooking.

questions.question: Which "Ratatouille" character illegally sells frozen food under the late chef's name?
questions.explanation: Sous chef Skinner secretly markets low-quality frozen meals branded with Gusteau's name for profit.

questions.question: What old film does the robot WALL-E repeatedly watch on a video cassette?
questions.explanation: WALL-E is captivated by the musical Hello, Dolly!, which shapes his longing for companionship.

questions.question: In "WALL-E," what secret directive does the autopilot AUTO follow to keep humans in space?
questions.explanation: AUTO secretly enforces Directive A113, ordering the ship never to return to Earth.

questions.question: What is the name of the South American destination Carl sets out to reach in "Up"?
questions.explanation: Carl ties thousands of balloons to his house to fulfill Ellie's dream of reaching Paradise Falls.

questions.question: Who is the disgraced explorer that Carl idolized as a boy in "Up"?
questions.explanation: Charles Muntz was Carl's childhood hero, later revealed to be a dangerous obsessive villain.

questions.question: In "Up," the giant bird nicknamed Kevin, thought to be male, turns out to be what?
questions.explanation: Kevin is revealed to be a mother bird searching for food to bring back to her chicks.

questions.question: In Brave, what skill does Merida practice at the start of the film?
questions.explanation: Merida is a gifted archer who cherishes her independence.

questions.question: In "Brave," what happens to Queen Elinor after she eats an enchanted cake?
questions.explanation: The witch's spell cake transforms Queen Elinor into a bear, forcing Merida to find a cure.

questions.question: Which of these is one of the three clans competing for Merida's hand in "Brave"?
questions.explanation: Clan Macintosh, along with Clan MacGuffin and Clan Dingwall, sends a son to compete for Merida.

questions.question: In "Inside Out," which emotion works the console alongside Joy, Sadness, Fear, and Anger?
questions.explanation: Disgust rounds out the five original emotions operating Riley's mind in Headquarters.

questions.question: In "Inside Out," which imaginary friend sacrifices himself in the Memory Dump to help Joy?
questions.explanation: Bing Bong fades away in the Memory Dump after boosting Joy's wagon out to safety.

questions.question: In "Inside Out," Riley and her family move from Minnesota to which city?
questions.explanation: The Andersen family's relocation from Minnesota to San Francisco upends Riley's emotional world.

questions.question: In "The Good Dinosaur," what species of dinosaur is the main character Arlo?
questions.explanation: Arlo is a young Apatosaurus who befriends a human boy named Spot.

questions.question: In Finding Dory, which institute does Dory search for her family?
questions.explanation: Dory's search leads her to the Marine Life Institute in California.

questions.question: In "Finding Dory," Hank the sea creature is missing a limb, making him what?
questions.explanation: Hank is technically a septopus, an octopus with only seven arms instead of eight.

questions.question: In "Cars 3," who is the sleek, high-tech rookie racer challenging Lightning McQueen?
questions.explanation: Jackson Storm represents a new generation of racers built with next-generation training technology.

questions.question: In "Coco," which character is revealed to be Miguel's true great-great-grandfather?
questions.explanation: Hector, not the famous Ernesto de la Cruz, turns out to be Miguel's real ancestor.

questions.question: What breed of hairless dog is Miguel's companion Dante in "Coco"?
questions.explanation: Dante is a Xoloitzcuintli, a hairless breed native to Mexico that guides Miguel in the spirit world.

questions.question: In "Incredibles 2," the villain Screenslaver is secretly which character?
questions.explanation: Tech mogul Evelyn Deavor is unmasked as the hypnotic villain Screenslaver.

questions.question: In "Incredibles 2," which new superhero idolizes Elastigirl and can create portals?
questions.explanation: Voyd is a starstruck new hero who admires Elastigirl and generates spatial portals.

questions.question: What is the surname of brothers Ian and Barley in "Onward"?
questions.explanation: Ian and Barley Lightfoot set out on a quest to briefly reunite with their late father.

questions.question: In "Onward," what magical item do Ian and Barley need to finish their father's spell?
questions.explanation: A rare Phoenix Gem is required to power the spell that would fully resurrect their father.

questions.question: In "Soul," Joe Gardner teaches middle school band and also performs as what?
questions.explanation: Joe Gardner dreams of a career as a jazz pianist beyond his day job teaching music.

questions.question: In "Soul," what is the name of the reluctant unborn soul who resists finding her spark?
questions.explanation: The soul known simply as 22 has avoided being born for eons before meeting Joe.

questions.question: In Luca, what are Luca and Alberto when they are in the water?
questions.explanation: Luca and Alberto are sea monsters who become human whenever they dry off.

questions.question: Who is the arrogant town bully and rival racer in "Luca"?
questions.explanation: Ercole Visconti repeatedly bullies Luca and Giulia while dominating the town's triathlon race.

questions.question: In "Turning Red," what triggers Mei Lee's transformation into a giant red panda?
questions.explanation: Mei transforms into a giant red panda whenever her emotions spike, echoing her ancestor's blessing.

questions.question: In Turning Red, in which Canadian city is the story set?
questions.explanation: Turning Red takes place in Toronto, Canada, in the early 2000s.

questions.question: In "Lightyear," what type of companion is Sox, given to Buzz by Alisha Hawthorne?
questions.explanation: Sox is a robotic cat companion assigned to Buzz that proves surprisingly resourceful.

questions.question: In "Lightyear," Emperor Zurg is ultimately revealed to be who?
answers: A future version of Buzz himself
questions.explanation: Zurg turns out to be an older Buzz Lightyear from an alternate timeline caused by time travel.

questions.question: In Elemental, which element is Wade, who befriends Ember?
questions.explanation: Wade is a gentle water person who falls for the fire element Ember.

questions.question: In "Elemental," what job does Wade Ripple hold when he first meets Ember?
questions.explanation: Wade works as an emotional city inspector who discovers a leak in Ember's family shop.

questions.question: Which new emotion becomes dominant in Riley's mind in "Inside Out 2"?
questions.explanation: Anxiety arrives during Riley's teenage years and quickly seizes control of Headquarters.

questions.question: What is the full name of the strawberry-scented bear villain in "Toy Story 3"?
questions.explanation: Lots-o'-Huggin' Bear, or Lotso, secretly rules Sunnyside Daycare with strict control.

questions.question: What is the name of the daycare center the toys are donated to in "Toy Story 3"?
questions.explanation: Sunnyside Daycare initially seems welcoming but proves to be run by the controlling Lotso.

questions.question: In "Toy Story 3," which oversized, cymbal-banging doll acts as Lotso's enforcer?
questions.explanation: Big Baby menacingly enforces Lotso's rule until he ultimately turns against him.

questions.question: What household item is transformed into a toy named Forky in "Toy Story 4"?
questions.explanation: Bonnie crafts a plastic spork into a googly-eyed toy she names Forky.

questions.question: In "Toy Story 4," what does the antique shop doll Gabby Gabby want from Woody?
questions.explanation: Gabby Gabby wants Woody's working voice box to replace her own broken one.

questions.question: In "Toy Story 4," Duke Caboom is a stuntman toy modeled after which daredevil persona?
questions.explanation: Duke Caboom is a discontinued toy styled as a boastful Canadian motorcycle stuntman.

questions.question: In "Monsters University," what is the name of the intimidating dean who doubts Mike?
questions.explanation: Dean Hardscrabble, a former top scarer, initially tells Mike he'll never be a scarer.

questions.question: What is the name of the underdog fraternity Mike and Sulley join in "Monsters University"?
questions.explanation: Oozma Kappa is the misfit fraternity that competes in the Scare Games alongside Mike and Sulley.

questions.question: In the Pixar short 'For the Birds,' what animals cause trouble on a wire?
questions.explanation: A flock of small birds mocks a larger bird perched on a telephone wire.

questions.question: The Pixar short "For the Birds" centers on small birds on a wire reacting to what?
answers: A large, ungainly bird joining them
questions.explanation: A flock of small songbirds mock and mistreat a big, gawky bird that lands beside them.

questions.question: In the Pixar short "Boundin'," a shorn sheep regains his confidence thanks to advice from what?
questions.explanation: A dancing jackalope teaches the embarrassed, freshly-sheared sheep to embrace his new look.

questions.question: In the Pixar short "Presto," a magician struggles to control what mischievous animal?
questions.explanation: Magician Presto battles his hungry rabbit Alec, who uses linked magic hats to get revenge.

questions.question: In the Pixar short "Partly Cloudy," a stork named Peck delivers babies created by what?
questions.explanation: Gus is a cloud who crafts unusual baby animals that his loyal stork friend Peck delivers.

questions.question: The Pixar short "La Luna" follows three generations of a family who work at what job?
questions.explanation: A grandfather, father, and son sail out nightly to sweep stars from the surface of the moon.

questions.question: The Pixar short "Lava" is a musical romance between two what?
questions.explanation: Lava tells the musical love story of two volcanoes, Uku and Lele, singing across the ocean.

questions.question: The Pixar short "Piper" follows a young sandpiper learning to do what?
answers: Forage for food in the surf
questions.explanation: A baby sandpiper overcomes her fear of the waves to learn to find clams in the surf.

questions.question: In the Pixar short "Bao," a dumpling that comes to life is a metaphor for what?
answers: An empty-nest parent's relationship with her child
questions.explanation: The animated dumpling represents a mother's struggle to let her grown child become independent.

questions.question: The Pixar short "Lou" features a creature living in a school lost-and-found bin made of what?
questions.explanation: Lou is a shapeshifting creature formed entirely from unclaimed items in the playground lost-and-found.

questions.question: In the SparkShorts film "Purl," a ball of yarn joins what kind of workplace?
questions.explanation: Purl navigates a bro-culture finance office as its only non-human, yarn-based employee.

questions.question: In Monsters, Inc., who is the company's top scarer at the start?
questions.explanation: Sulley is Monsters, Inc.'s leading scarer before he befriends the child Boo.

questions.question: What alphanumeric code, a nod to a CalArts classroom, is hidden throughout many Pixar films?
questions.explanation: A113 references a classroom used by CalArts animation students and recurs as a Pixar in-joke.

questions.question: In the "Toy Story" films, what evil emperor does Buzz Lightyear believe is his nemesis?
questions.explanation: Buzz's cartoon backstory casts Emperor Zurg as his sworn intergalactic enemy.

questions.question: In "Toy Story 2," which specialist repairs Woody's torn arm before a planned museum sale?
questions.explanation: Al hires a toy restorer known as the Cleaner to fix Woody's arm and repaint him.

questions.question: In "Toy Story 2," which "Woody's Roundup" toy has never been removed from his box?
questions.explanation: Stinky Pete remained mint-in-box for decades and schemes to stay that way permanently.

questions.question: In "Toy Story 2," which toy has a broken squeaker and is nearly sold at a yard sale?
questions.explanation: Wheezy, a squeeze-toy penguin, loses his squeak and is set aside for a yard sale.

questions.question: In "A Bug's Life," what must the ants gather each season to appease the grasshoppers?
questions.explanation: The ants must collect a large food offering annually or face the grasshoppers' wrath.

questions.question: In "A Bug's Life," Tuck and Roll are twin circus performers belonging to which insect type?
questions.explanation: Tuck and Roll are a pair of squabbling Hungarian pillbugs in P.T. Flea's circus.

questions.question: In "Monsters, Inc.," what facility stores millions of doors used to reach children's bedrooms?
questions.explanation: The Door Vault houses the massive collection of doors that scarers use to enter kids' rooms.

questions.question: What is Sulley's full name in "Monsters, Inc."?
questions.explanation: Sulley is formally known as James P. Sullivan, Monsters, Inc.'s top scarer.

questions.question: In "Monsters, Inc.," what type of creature is Mike Wazowski's girlfriend Celia?
questions.explanation: Celia has living snakes for hair, giving her a Gorgon-inspired monster design.

questions.question: In "Finding Nemo," how many of Marlin and Coral's eggs survive the barracuda attack?
questions.explanation: A barracuda attack kills Coral and destroys the entire clutch of eggs except for Nemo.

questions.question: In "Finding Nemo," which bird helps Marlin and later befriends Nemo at the dentist's office?
questions.explanation: Nigel the pelican relays news to Marlin and keeps Nemo company inside the dentist's fish tank.

questions.question: In "The Incredibles," what is the name of Syndrome's learning robot built to fight supers?
questions.explanation: The Omnidroid is a self-adapting robot Syndrome designs and later stages as a public threat.

questions.question: In "The Incredibles," who is Syndrome's sophisticated assistant who later helps the Parr family?
questions.explanation: Mirage works for Syndrome but ultimately turns against him to save the captured Parr family.

questions.question: In what city is the restaurant Gusteau's located in "Ratatouille"?
questions.explanation: Gusteau's, the once five-star restaurant central to the story, is located in Paris.

questions.question: What is the profession of Anton Ego, the imposing figure who reviews Linguini's cooking?
questions.explanation: Anton Ego is a feared, influential food critic whose review can make or break a restaurant.

questions.question: In "WALL-E," what is the title of the character commanding the starliner Axiom?
questions.explanation: Captain McCrea commands the Axiom and ultimately fights AUTO to steer humanity back to Earth.

questions.question: What is the name of the massive starliner where humanity lives in "WALL-E"?
questions.explanation: Humanity has lived aboard the starliner Axiom for centuries after abandoning a polluted Earth.

questions.question: In "Up," Russell is a Wilderness Explorer trying to earn what badge by helping Carl?
questions.explanation: Russell tags along with Carl hoping to earn his final badge for assisting the elderly.

questions.question: In "Up," what device allows the dog Dug to speak in English?
questions.explanation: Dug wears a specialized collar invented by Muntz that translates his thoughts into speech.

questions.question: In "Brave," what was the demon bear Mor'du before his transformation?
answers: A prince who sought a spell to change his fate
questions.explanation: Mor'du was once a prince who asked the witch for a spell and became trapped as a bear forever.

questions.question: In "Brave," what glowing spirits lead Merida to the witch's cottage?
questions.explanation: Merida follows mysterious blue will o' the wisps that are said to lead to one's fate.

questions.question: In "Inside Out," what is the unsettling clown figure in Riley's subconscious called?
questions.explanation: Jangles the Clown is a forgotten, frightening memory locked away in Riley's subconscious.

questions.question: In "The Good Dinosaur," Arlo's father Poppa Henry dies trying to save Arlo from what?
questions.explanation: Poppa Henry is swept away and killed while rescuing Arlo during a sudden flash flood.

questions.question: In "Finding Dory," nearsighted whale shark Destiny communicates through a pipe with which beluga?
questions.explanation: Destiny and the self-doubting beluga Bailey are old tank-mates who talk through connecting pipes.

questions.question: In "Cars 3," Cruz Ramirez initially works for Lightning McQueen as his what?
questions.explanation: Cruz Ramirez starts out as a young trainer hired to help McQueen prepare for a comeback.

questions.question: In "Coco," what dark secret is eventually revealed about musician Ernesto de la Cruz?
answers: He murdered Hector to steal his songs
He was never a real musician
questions.explanation: Ernesto de la Cruz poisoned his songwriting partner Hector and claimed his songs as his own.

questions.question: In "Coco," Mama Imelda's spirit guide Pepita takes the form of what kind of alebrije?
questions.explanation: Pepita is Imelda's fierce spirit animal guide, resembling a winged jaguar in alebrije form.

questions.question: In "Turning Red," the Lee family's red panda blessing traces back to which ancestor?
questions.explanation: The family temple honors ancestor Sun Yee, whose ancient blessing grants the red panda transformation.

questions.question: Which hopping desk lamp, star of Pixar's first short, appears in the studio's opening logo?
questions.explanation: Luxo Jr., from Pixar's 1986 short of the same name, became the mascot in Pixar's logo.

questions.question: In 'Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs,' what does the Magic Mirror call Snow White?
questions.explanation: The Magic Mirror repeatedly tells the Evil Queen that Snow White is the fairest in the land.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: How many dwarfs share their forest cottage with Snow White?
questions.explanation: Snow White lives with seven dwarfs named Doc, Grumpy, Happy, Sleepy, Bashful, Sneezy, and Dopey.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Which dwarf is known for his grouchy, gruff personality?
questions.explanation: Grumpy is the dwarf who grumbles and acts gruff, though he cares deeply for Snow White.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: Who is the villain that orders Snow White's heart be cut out?
questions.explanation: The jealous Evil Queen wants Snow White killed because she envies her beauty.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What magical item does the Evil Queen consult to learn who is fairest of all?
questions.explanation: The Evil Queen's enchanted mirror truthfully answers her questions about beauty.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What poisoned fruit does the Evil Queen trick Snow White into eating?
questions.explanation: Disguised as an old peddler woman, the Evil Queen gives Snow White a poisoned apple.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: In which song does Snow White wish for love while standing at a wishing well?
answers: A Dream Is a Wish Your Heart Makes
questions.explanation: Snow White sings 'Someday My Prince Will Come' while wishing for love at the wishing well.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: How is Snow White finally revived at the end of the film?
questions.explanation: The Prince's kiss breaks the sleeping curse and Snow White awakens.
questions.sourceTitle: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs

questions.question: What does Cinderella lose on the palace steps as she flees at midnight?
questions.explanation: Cinderella loses one glass slipper while rushing home before the Fairy Godmother's magic ends.

questions.question: What are the names of Cinderella's two closest mouse friends?
questions.explanation: Jaq and Gus are the loyal mice who help Cinderella with her chores and dress.

questions.question: Who magically prepares Cinderella for the royal ball?
questions.explanation: Cinderella's Fairy Godmother appears and uses magic to send her to the ball.

questions.question: In Cinderella, what does the Fairy Godmother turn into a coach?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother magically transforms an ordinary pumpkin into a grand coach.

questions.question: What are the names of Cinderella's two stepsisters?
questions.explanation: Anastasia and Drizella are Lady Tremaine's daughters, who treat Cinderella poorly.

questions.question: What vegetable does the Fairy Godmother turn into a grand carriage?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother transforms a pumpkin into Cinderella's carriage for the ball.

questions.question: By what time must Cinderella return home before the magic spell breaks?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother warns Cinderella that all her magic disappears at the stroke of midnight.

questions.question: What magic phrase does the Fairy Godmother sing while waving her wand?
questions.explanation: The Fairy Godmother sings 'Bibbidi-Bobbidi-Boo' as she casts her spells.

questions.question: What name do the three fairies use to hide Princess Aurora while raising her?
questions.explanation: To keep her safe from Maleficent, Aurora is raised in secret as 'Briar Rose.'

questions.question: What are the names of the three good fairies who protect Aurora?
questions.explanation: Flora, Fauna, and Merryweather raise Aurora in the forest to protect her from the curse.

questions.question: What is the name of Prince Phillip's loyal horse?
questions.explanation: Samson is Prince Phillip's horse who helps him battle Maleficent's forces.

questions.question: Which villain curses baby Aurora to prick her finger and die?
questions.explanation: Maleficent, angry at not being invited to the christening, curses Aurora at her birth.

questions.question: Which king is Princess Aurora's father?
questions.explanation: King Stefan and Queen Leah are Princess Aurora's parents.

questions.question: What object pricks Aurora's finger and causes her to fall under the curse?
questions.explanation: Aurora pricks her finger on an enchanted spinning wheel's spindle, fulfilling the curse.

questions.question: What song do Aurora and Prince Phillip sing when they first meet in the forest?
questions.explanation: Aurora and Phillip sing 'Once Upon a Dream' during their chance meeting in the woods.

questions.question: What does Ariel keep hidden in her secret grotto?
questions.explanation: Ariel collects human objects from shipwrecks and hides them in her secret underwater grotto.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Flounder, Ariel's best friend?
questions.explanation: Flounder is a small yellow-and-blue fish who is Ariel's loyal best friend.

questions.question: Which crab is King Triton's court composer and Ariel's reluctant guardian?
questions.explanation: Sebastian the crab is tasked with keeping an eye on Ariel while serving as court composer.

questions.question: Who is the sea witch that tricks Ariel into a dangerous magical bargain?
questions.explanation: Ursula the sea witch offers Ariel legs in exchange for her beautiful voice.

questions.question: Who is Ariel's father, the ruler of the sea?
questions.explanation: King Triton rules the underwater kingdom and is Ariel's protective father.

questions.question: What is the name of the undersea kingdom where Ariel lives?
questions.explanation: Ariel is a mermaid princess of the underwater kingdom of Atlantica.

questions.question: What does Ariel give up in exchange for human legs?
questions.explanation: Ariel trades her beautiful singing voice to Ursula in exchange for human legs.

questions.question: How many days does Ariel have to receive true love's kiss in Ursula's bargain?
questions.explanation: Ursula's contract gives Ariel three days to earn a kiss of true love or belong to Ursula.

questions.question: In which song does Ariel dream about living among humans on land?
questions.explanation: Ariel sings 'Part of Your World' while longing to leave the sea and join the human world.

questions.question: What does Belle enjoy doing most in her quiet French village?
questions.explanation: Belle is known throughout her village for always having her nose in a book.

questions.question: What household object has the servant Lumiere been enchanted into?
questions.explanation: Lumiere the charming servant is transformed into a candelabra by the enchantress's curse.

questions.question: What magical object lets the Beast see anywhere in the world, including Belle's village?
questions.explanation: The Beast owns an enchanted mirror that can show him any place he wishes to see.

questions.question: What happens to the castle's servants once the Beast's curse is finally broken?
questions.explanation: When the spell breaks, Lumiere, Cogsworth, Mrs. Potts, and the other servants become human again.

questions.question: Who is the arrogant hunter determined to marry Belle?
questions.explanation: Gaston is the vain, boastful hunter who wants Belle for his wife.

questions.question: What is the name of Belle's inventor father?
questions.explanation: Maurice is Belle's kind but eccentric inventor father.

questions.question: What enchanted flower shows how much time the Beast has left to break his curse?
questions.explanation: The enchanted rose loses a petal as time runs out for the Beast to learn to love.

questions.question: Why does Belle agree to remain a prisoner in the Beast's castle?
questions.explanation: Belle offers herself as prisoner in exchange for her father Maurice's freedom.

questions.question: In which song does the enchanted castle staff welcome Belle to a lavish dinner?
questions.explanation: Lumiere and the castle staff perform 'Be Our Guest' to welcome Belle.

questions.question: Which princess longs to see the world beyond her palace in Agrabah?
questions.explanation: Princess Jasmine dreams of freedom beyond her palace walls in Aladdin.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Abu, Aladdin's mischievous companion?
questions.explanation: Abu is a small, thieving monkey who travels everywhere with Aladdin.

questions.question: Who grants Aladdin three wishes after he rubs a magic lamp?
questions.explanation: A powerful Genie emerges from the magic lamp and grants Aladdin three wishes.

questions.question: Who is the Sultan's power-hungry royal vizier and the film's main villain?
questions.explanation: Jafar schemes to seize the throne of Agrabah using dark magic.

questions.question: Who is Princess Jasmine's father, ruler of Agrabah?
questions.explanation: The Sultan is Jasmine's father and the kind but easily manipulated ruler of Agrabah.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom where Aladdin and Jasmine live?
questions.explanation: Agrabah is the fictional Middle Eastern kingdom that serves as the setting for 'Aladdin.'

questions.question: What object does Aladdin retrieve from the Cave of Wonders that houses a genie?
questions.explanation: Aladdin finds an old oil lamp in the Cave of Wonders that contains a powerful genie.

questions.question: What song plays as Aladdin and Jasmine fly together over the city at night?
questions.explanation: Aladdin and Jasmine share a magic carpet ride while singing 'A Whole New World.'

questions.question: What does Pocahontas enjoy leaping from into the river near her village?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas is known for her adventurous spirit, including diving from a high cliff.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Meeko, Pocahontas's mischievous friend?
questions.explanation: Meeko is a playful, food-loving raccoon who accompanies Pocahontas.

questions.question: What ancient talking tree gives Pocahontas wise advice?
questions.explanation: Grandmother Willow is a wise, spiritual tree who guides Pocahontas.

questions.question: What is the name of the greedy English governor who leads the settlers?
questions.explanation: Governor Ratcliffe leads the English settlers and searches obsessively for gold.

questions.question: Who is Pocahontas's father, the chief of her tribe?
questions.explanation: Chief Powhatan leads his people and is Pocahontas's father.

questions.question: In which song does Pocahontas describe the beauty and spirit of nature to John Smith?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas sings 'Colors of the Wind' to teach John Smith about respecting nature.

questions.question: What does Mulan disguise herself as in order to take her father's place in the army?
questions.explanation: Mulan cuts her hair and dresses as a man to secretly join the army in her father's place.

questions.question: What small, talkative dragon becomes Mulan's guardian companion?
questions.explanation: Mushu is the tiny dragon who accompanies Mulan and tries to help her succeed.

questions.question: In which country does the story of 'Mulan' take place?
questions.explanation: Mulan is set in ancient China, where she joins the army to defend her homeland.

questions.question: Who leads the invading army that threatens China in the film?
questions.explanation: Shan Yu commands the Huns who invade China, prompting the Emperor's call to arms.

questions.question: What is the name of Mulan's father, the aging soldier she wants to protect?
questions.explanation: Fa Zhou is Mulan's father, whom she secretly replaces in the army to spare him from battle.

questions.question: What false name does Mulan use while disguised as a soldier?
questions.explanation: Mulan adopts the name 'Ping' to hide her identity while training as a soldier.

questions.question: Which song plays while Mulan and the other recruits train to become soldiers?
answers: I'll Make a Man Out of You
questions.explanation: 'I'll Make a Man Out of You' is sung by Captain Li Shang during the recruits' training montage.

questions.question: What is Tiana's biggest dream while working hard in New Orleans?
questions.explanation: Tiana dreams of opening her own restaurant, inspired by her late father's ambition.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Louis, who dreams of playing jazz trumpet?
questions.explanation: Louis is a friendly alligator who loves music and dreams of performing jazz.

questions.question: What is the name of the lovestruck firefly who helps Tiana and Naveen?
questions.explanation: Ray the Cajun firefly befriends Tiana and Naveen and guides them through the bayou.

questions.question: Who is the sinister voodoo man who turns Prince Naveen into a frog?
questions.explanation: Dr. Facilier uses dark magic to transform Prince Naveen into a frog.

questions.question: What is the name of Tiana's hardworking father, who shared her love of cooking?
questions.explanation: James is Tiana's father, whose dream of owning a restaurant inspires her own.

questions.question: In which American city is 'The Princess and the Frog' set?
questions.explanation: The film takes place in and around New Orleans during the 1920s.

questions.question: What happens to Tiana right after she kisses the frog Prince Naveen?
answers: She turns into a frog too
questions.explanation: Instead of breaking the spell, Tiana's kiss causes her to become a frog as well.

questions.question: What is unique about Rapunzel's incredibly long hair?
answers: It changes color with her mood
It can turn into rope instantly
It glows and has healing magic
questions.explanation: Rapunzel's magical golden hair glows and can heal wounds or reverse aging when she sings.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Pascal, Rapunzel's loyal companion?
questions.explanation: Pascal is a color-changing chameleon who stays by Rapunzel's side throughout her tower years.

questions.question: What is the name of the persistent palace horse who chases Flynn Rider?
questions.explanation: Maximus is the loyal, determined horse who pursues Flynn Rider before joining the adventure.

questions.question: Who kidnaps baby Rapunzel and raises her in a hidden tower?
questions.explanation: Mother Gothel steals Rapunzel as a baby to keep her magical hair for herself.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom that Rapunzel is the lost princess of?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel is revealed to be the long-lost princess of the kingdom of Corona.

questions.question: What household item does Rapunzel skillfully use as a weapon throughout the film?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel wields a heavy frying pan to defend herself throughout her journey.

questions.question: What yearly event do Rapunzel and Flynn watch together, filled with floating lights?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel and Flynn watch thousands of floating lanterns released during the kingdom's yearly festival.

questions.question: In which song do Rapunzel and Flynn watch the floating lanterns from a boat?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel and Flynn share a tender moment during the song 'I See the Light.'

questions.question: What skill is Merida especially talented at, as shown in an archery contest?
questions.explanation: Merida is a highly skilled archer who competes in the contest for her own hand in marriage.

questions.question: What is the name of Merida's loyal horse?
questions.explanation: Angus is Merida's clever and loyal Clydesdale horse.

questions.question: Who is Merida's father, the boisterous king of DunBroch?
questions.explanation: King Fergus is Merida's loud, good-natured father and ruler of DunBroch.

questions.question: What happens to Queen Elinor after Merida gives her an enchanted cake?
answers: She falls into a deep sleep
questions.explanation: A witch's spell hidden in the cake accidentally transforms Queen Elinor into a bear.

questions.question: What is the name of the Scottish kingdom ruled by Merida's family?
questions.explanation: DunBroch is the Scottish kingdom ruled by King Fergus and Queen Elinor.

questions.question: Which song plays as Merida rides freely through the highlands on Angus?
questions.explanation: 'Touch the Sky' plays as Merida rides through the Scottish highlands, celebrating her freedom.

questions.question: What role is Moana destined to inherit within her village?
questions.explanation: As the chief's daughter, Moana is destined to one day lead her people as chief.

questions.question: What kind of animal is Pua, Moana's pet who stays behind on the island?
questions.explanation: Pua is Moana's loyal pet pig who remains on Motunui during her voyage.

questions.question: What demigod does Moana recruit to help restore the Heart of Te Fiti?
questions.explanation: Moana seeks out the demigod Maui, who once stole the Heart of Te Fiti.

questions.question: What must Moana do to save her island from a mysterious blight?
answers: Build a new fleet of canoes
Sail beyond the reef to restore the heart

She falls into an enchanted sleep

questions.question: In Frozen, which elder troll heals young Anna after Elsa's magic strikes her?

answers: She is the goddess Te Fiti, missing her heart

questions.question: What is the name of Moana's home island, which she is destined to one day lead?

questions.explanation: Mal, the lead character of Descendants, is the daughter of the villainess Maleficent.

questions.question: In Twitches, what do the twin sisters Alex and Camryn discover they are on their 21st birthday?
questions.explanation: Reunited twins Alex and Camryn learn they possess magical witch powers tied to another world.

questions.question: In High School Musical 2, where do Troy and his friends get summer jobs?
questions.explanation: The East High gang spends their summer working at the Lava Springs country club in High School Musical 2.

questions.question: In the 1998 version of The Parent Trap, which actress plays both twin sisters?
questions.explanation: Lindsay Lohan plays both separated twins, Hallie and Annie, in the 1998 remake of The Parent Trap.

questions.question: In Swiss Family Robinson, what type of home does the shipwrecked family build?
questions.explanation: After being shipwrecked, the Robinson family constructs an elaborate treehouse on their island.

questions.question: In Bedknobs and Broomsticks, what is the name of the apprentice witch played by Angela Lansbury?
questions.explanation: Angela Lansbury plays Eglantine Price, an amateur witch training by correspondence course.

questions.question: What is the name of the sentient Volkswagen Beetle race car in The Love Bug?
questions.explanation: Herbie is the lovable, independent-minded Volkswagen Beetle at the heart of The Love Bug.

questions.question: In Pollyanna, what game does the optimistic title character teach the townspeople to play?
questions.explanation: Pollyanna teaches others to play the Glad Game, finding something to be glad about in every situation.

questions.question: What rubbery, energy-giving substance does the scientist invent in The Absent-Minded Professor?
questions.explanation: Professor Ned Brainard accidentally invents Flubber, a flying rubber substance, in The Absent-Minded Professor.

questions.question: In That Darn Cat, what does the title cat named D.C. help the FBI solve?
questions.explanation: The mischievous cat D.C. inadvertently helps FBI agents crack a kidnapping case.

questions.question: In 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea, who commands the submarine Nautilus?
questions.explanation: The mysterious Captain Nemo commands the advanced submarine Nautilus in 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea.

questions.question: In the original Freaky Friday films, what unusual event happens between a mother and daughter?
questions.explanation: A magical mix-up causes a mother and daughter to switch bodies for a day in Freaky Friday.

questions.question: In Old Yeller, what type of dog is the family's loyal companion, Old Yeller?
questions.explanation: Old Yeller is a scruffy stray dog who becomes a beloved and loyal member of the Coates family.

questions.question: Remember the Titans is based on the true story of a football team doing what for the first time?
questions.explanation: Remember the Titans dramatizes a Virginia high school football team's real racial integration in 1971.

questions.question: The film Miracle recounts the true story of which underdog sports achievement?
answers: The 1994 US Olympic figure skating team
questions.explanation: Miracle tells the true story of the 1980 US Olympic hockey team's upset win over the Soviet Union.

questions.question: The Rookie is based on the true story of Jim Morris, a man who became a professional what?
questions.explanation: The Rookie follows the real story of Jim Morris, a teacher who became a Major League pitcher in his 30s.

questions.question: In Holes, what is the name of the main character sentenced to dig holes at Camp Green Lake?
questions.explanation: Stanley Yelnats is wrongly sent to Camp Green Lake, where he is forced to dig holes as punishment.

questions.question: In Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time, what magical object allows its user to rewind time?
questions.explanation: The Dagger of Time allows its user to rewind time for short periods in Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time.
questions.sourceTitle: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time

questions.question: John Carter is based on novels by which author, largely set on Mars?
questions.explanation: John Carter adapts Edgar Rice Burroughs's Barsoom novels, in which a Civil War veteran is transported to Mars.

questions.question: In The Sorcerer's Apprentice, what does Nicolas Cage's character Balthazar Blake seek in modern New York?
questions.explanation: Balthazar Blake seeks a suitable apprentice to help him defeat an ancient evil sorceress.

questions.question: In Holes, what nickname is given to Stanley's quiet friend at Camp Green Lake?
questions.explanation: Stanley befriends a quiet boy nicknamed Zero, who becomes central to solving the story's mystery.

questions.question: In the live-action Cinderella (2015), which actress plays the wicked stepmother?
questions.explanation: Cate Blanchett portrays Lady Tremaine, Cinderella's cruel stepmother, in the 2015 live-action film.

questions.question: In the live-action version of The Jungle Book, what animals raise the boy Mowgli?
questions.explanation: Mowgli is raised by a wolf pack led by Raksha in the live-action Jungle Book.

questions.question: Who plays Belle in the live-action Beauty and the Beast (2017)?
questions.explanation: Emma Watson stars as Belle in Disney's 2017 live-action retelling of Beauty and the Beast.

questions.question: Which actor voices the Genie in the live-action Aladdin (2019)?
questions.explanation: Will Smith plays the Genie in Disney's 2019 live-action remake of Aladdin.

questions.question: Who directed the 2019 live-action remake of Dumbo?
questions.explanation: Tim Burton directed the 2019 live-action reimagining of Dumbo.

questions.question: In the live-action Maleficent, whose perspective retells the Sleeping Beauty story?
questions.explanation: Maleficent reimagines the Sleeping Beauty tale from the point of view of the horned villain herself.

questions.question: In Pete's Dragon (2016), what is the name of the dragon that befriends Pete?
questions.explanation: Pete's loyal, furry green dragon companion in the live-action remake is named Elliot.

questions.question: In the live-action 101 Dalmatians, who is the fur-obsessed villain trying to steal the puppies?
questions.explanation: Cruella de Vil schemes to steal Dalmatian puppies for a fur coat in the live-action 101 Dalmatians.

questions.question: In Christopher Robin, why does the grown-up Christopher Robin return to the Hundred Acre Wood?
questions.explanation: An adult, overworked Christopher Robin is drawn back when his old friend Pooh Bear comes looking for him.

questions.question: In the 2019 live-action Lion King, what technique was primarily used to create the animals?
questions.explanation: The 2019 Lion King used photorealistic CGI to create lifelike digital animals in a live-action style.

questions.question: In Enchanted, what is the name of the animated princess who is transported to real-life New York City?
questions.explanation: Giselle is whisked from her animated fairy-tale world into modern-day New York City in Enchanted.

questions.question: In Alice in Wonderland (2010), what is the full name of the Mad Hatter, played by Johnny Depp?
questions.explanation: Johnny Depp's Mad Hatter is given the full name Tarrant Hightopp in Tim Burton's Alice in Wonderland.

questions.question: In Cruella, what is the childhood name of the girl who grows up to become Cruella de Vil?
questions.explanation: Cruella de Vil is originally named Estella before adopting her villainous alter ego.

questions.question: In Hocus Pocus, what are the three resurrected witch sisters collectively known as?
questions.explanation: Winifred, Sarah, and Mary Sanderson are collectively known as the Sanderson sisters in Hocus Pocus.

questions.question: What event forces Ben Gates and his team to steal the Declaration of Independence in National Treasure?
answers: Preventing rival treasure hunters from destroying it
questions.explanation: Gates steals the Declaration to keep it safe from Ian Howe's crew, who would destroy it to reach the clue.

questions.question: What event kidnaps Elizabeth Swann and kicks off the plot in Curse of the Black Pearl?
answers: She challenges Barbossa to a duel
She is mistaken for a Navy spy
Barbossa's crew mistakes her medallion for cursed gold
questions.explanation: Barbossa's cursed crew kidnaps Elizabeth after recognizing the pirate medallion she wears.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

questions.question: In Mary Poppins, what event at the bank triggers a run on Fidelity Fiduciary Bank?
answers: Michael Banks refuses to hand over his tuppence and causes an uproar
Mary Poppins withdraws all the family's savings
questions.explanation: Michael's cry to keep his tuppence sparks panic among customers, triggering a run on the bank.

questions.question: In The Curse of the Black Pearl, what cursed treasure must be returned?
questions.explanation: The crew must return the cursed Aztec gold pieces to break their skeletal curse.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

questions.question: What is the name of Davy Jones's ship in the Pirates of the Caribbean series?
questions.explanation: Davy Jones commands the ghostly Flying Dutchman, ferrying souls lost at sea to the next world.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest

questions.question: In Dead Man's Chest, which ship is Bootstrap Bill Turner cursed to serve upon?
questions.explanation: Bootstrap Bill was bound to Davy Jones's crew aboard the Flying Dutchman after being thrown overboard.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest

questions.question: At the end of At World's End, which character is elected Pirate King?
questions.explanation: Elizabeth Swann is unanimously voted Pirate King at the Brethren Court meeting on Shipwreck Island.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End

questions.question: In Dead Man's Chest, on which island is Davy Jones's buried heart dug up?
questions.explanation: Jack Sparrow and Will Turner race rival crews to unearth the buried chest on Isla Cruces.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest

questions.question: Which sea goddess is revealed to be trapped in the human form of Tia Dalma?
questions.explanation: Tia Dalma is revealed to be the sea goddess Calypso, bound into human form by the pirate lords.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest

questions.question: In On Stranger Tides, what legendary place do Blackbeard and Jack seek?
questions.explanation: On Stranger Tides follows a race to reach the Fountain of Youth.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides

questions.question: In On Stranger Tides, Angelica is revealed to be whose daughter?
questions.explanation: Angelica is revealed to be Blackbeard's daughter, complicating her romance with Jack Sparrow.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides

questions.question: What legendary site do Jack Sparrow and Blackbeard's crew seek in On Stranger Tides?
questions.explanation: Both crews race to find the Fountain of Youth, requiring a mermaid's tear for its ritual.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides

questions.question: What is the name of Captain Salazar's ghost ship in Dead Men Tell No Tales?
questions.explanation: Captain Salazar commands the Silent Mary, a ghost ship crewed by his undead Spanish sailors.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales

questions.question: In Dead Men Tell No Tales, Henry Turner searches for which artifact to break a family curse?
questions.explanation: Henry Turner seeks the Trident of Poseidon, believing it can free his father from the Dutchman's curse.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales

questions.question: What object does Jack Sparrow carry that points toward what its holder desires most?
questions.explanation: Jack's unusual compass does not point north but instead toward whatever its holder wants most.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

questions.question: In Mary Poppins, what is Mr. George Banks's occupation?
questions.explanation: George Banks works at a London bank and prizes order and routine.

questions.question: What is the name of the bank where George Banks works in Mary Poppins?
questions.explanation: George Banks is a senior clerk at the Fidelity Fiduciary Bank, where Michael's tuppence causes a run.

questions.question: What job does Bert have during the rooftop "Chim Chim Cher-ee" number in Mary Poppins?
questions.explanation: Bert leads a troupe of chimney sweeps across the rooftops of London during this famous dance number.

questions.question: Who is the Banks family's neighbor who fires a cannon each day from his rooftop?
questions.explanation: Admiral Boom, a retired naval officer, fires a rooftop cannon at set times every day on Cherry Tree Lane.

questions.question: What happens to Uncle Albert whenever he laughs in Mary Poppins?
questions.explanation: Uncle Albert floats up to the ceiling whenever he laughs, and only sad thoughts bring him back down.

questions.question: In Mary Poppins Returns, during which economic era is the story set?
questions.explanation: The sequel is set decades later in Depression-era London, as the grown Banks children face financial hardship.

questions.question: What is the name of the villainous banker in Mary Poppins Returns?
questions.explanation: William Weatherall Wilkins schemes to seize the Banks family home over an unpaid loan at the bank.

questions.question: What are the names of Michael Banks's three children in Mary Poppins Returns?
questions.explanation: Michael Banks's children, Annabel, John, and Georgie, join Mary Poppins on new adventures around London.

questions.question: In The Muppet Movie, what city is Kermit's ultimate destination as he travels cross-country?
questions.explanation: Kermit sets out for Hollywood hoping to make millions of people happy by becoming a movie star.

questions.question: In The Muppet Christmas Carol, which Muppet narrates as Charles Dickens?
questions.explanation: Gonzo narrates the story as Charles Dickens, joined by Rizzo the Rat.

questions.question: Who plays Long John Silver in Muppet Treasure Island?
questions.explanation: Tim Curry stars as the cunning ship's cook and pirate Long John Silver in this Muppet adaptation.

questions.question: What are the names of the two grumpy hecklers who watch Muppet shows from a balcony?
questions.explanation: Statler and Waldorf are the elderly duo who mock the performances from their balcony box seats.

questions.question: In The Muppets (2011), what is the name of the tycoon who plans to demolish the Muppet Studios?
questions.explanation: Oil tycoon Tex Richman wants to drill for oil beneath the old Muppet Studios lot in this film.

questions.question: In Muppets Most Wanted, what is the name of Kermit's criminal look-alike?
questions.explanation: The jewel thief Constantine impersonates Kermit while the real frog is imprisoned in a Siberian gulag.

questions.question: In Muppets from Space, Gonzo searches for what, since his species is famously unknown?
questions.explanation: Because no one knows what species Gonzo is, this film follows his quest to learn where he came from.

questions.question: What is the name of the wisecracking rat who is a frequent Muppet castmate of Miss Piggy?
questions.explanation: Rizzo the Rat is a recurring Muppet performer known for his sarcastic humor and love of food.

questions.question: In the original Tron, what company did programmer Kevin Flynn used to work for?
questions.explanation: Kevin Flynn was a programmer at ENCOM before being pulled into the digital world he helped create.

questions.question: What is the name of the tyrannical program that rules the digital world in the original Tron?
questions.explanation: The Master Control Program has seized control of the digital grid and enslaves other programs to serve it.

questions.question: In Tron: Legacy, what is the name of Kevin Flynn's son who enters the Grid?
questions.explanation: Sam Flynn investigates his missing father's old arcade and is pulled into the digital Grid himself.

questions.question: In Tron: Legacy, which rogue program modeled after Kevin Flynn seizes control of the Grid?
questions.explanation: CLU, a digital copy of a younger Flynn, rebels and traps his creator inside the Grid for decades.

questions.question: What is the name of the security program, based on Alan Bradley, who aids Flynn in Tron?
questions.explanation: The program Tron, based on user Alan Bradley, fights alongside Flynn to defeat the Master Control Program.

questions.question: In Tron: Legacy, Quorra is revealed to be what kind of unique digital being?
questions.explanation: Quorra is an ISO, a rare self-generated life form that CLU sought to eliminate from the Grid.

questions.question: What high-speed vehicles do programs race along light trails in the Tron films?
questions.explanation: Light cycles leave solid trails of light behind them, used both for racing and deadly combat games.

questions.question: What is the name of Nicolas Cage's treasure-hunting character in National Treasure?
questions.explanation: Benjamin Franklin Gates comes from a long line of treasure hunters pursuing a legendary Templar fortune.

questions.question: In National Treasure, what founding document does Ben Gates take to protect it from thieves?
questions.explanation: Ben Gates steals the Declaration of Independence to keep it safe and uncover a hidden treasure map.

questions.question: What is the name of Ben Gates's tech-savvy sidekick in the National Treasure films?
questions.explanation: Riley Poole is Ben Gates's computer expert friend who assists on every treasure hunt in the series.

questions.question: Who is the primary villain in the first National Treasure film?
questions.explanation: Ian Howe, a former ally turned rival, funds the expedition before betraying Ben to claim the treasure alone.

questions.question: In National Treasure: Book of Secrets, what lost city of gold do the characters search for?
questions.explanation: The sequel's treasure hunt leads Ben Gates and his team toward the legendary golden city of Cibola.

questions.question: In Book of Secrets, what historical event is Ben Gates's ancestor accused of being involved in?
answers: The burning of the White House
questions.explanation: A newly found page suggests Ben's ancestor conspired in Lincoln's assassination, spurring the sequel's quest.

questions.question: What is the name of the archivist and love interest who joins Ben Gates on his quests?
questions.explanation: Abigail Chase, a National Archives conservator, joins Ben's team and becomes his romantic interest.

questions.question: In Book of Secrets, which historic estate holds a clue about the president's secret book?
questions.explanation: The team searches George Washington's estate, Mount Vernon, for clues tied to the presidents' secret book.

questions.question: In High School Musical, what is the name of Troy and Gabriella's high school?
questions.explanation: Troy Bolton and Gabriella Montez attend East High, home of the Wildcats basketball team.

questions.question: In Descendants, what is the name of the isolated island where villains' children grow up?
questions.explanation: The children of Disney villains are raised on the Isle of the Lost, cut off from the kingdom of Auradon.

questions.question: In Descendants, what is the name of the island where villains are exiled?
questions.explanation: The villains and their children are banished to the Isle of the Lost.

questions.question: What is the name of the summer camp for young musicians in Camp Rock?
questions.explanation: Mitchie Torres attends Camp Rock, a summer camp where aspiring young musicians hone their talents.

questions.question: In Teen Beach Movie, the main characters are transported into which type of film?
questions.explanation: Surfing teens Brady and Mackenzie are pulled into the world of a fictional 1960s beach party film.

questions.question: In High School Musical 2, where do Troy and his friends spend the summer working?
questions.explanation: The East High gang takes summer jobs at Lava Springs Country Club, owned by Sharpay's family.

questions.question: In Halloweentown, what is the name of the young protagonist who discovers she's a witch?
questions.explanation: Marnie Piper learns her family has magical roots after visiting her grandmother in Halloweentown.

questions.question: How many members make up the singing group in The Cheetah Girls?
questions.explanation: The Cheetah Girls is a group of four friends who dream of becoming a famous singing act.

questions.question: Who plays the title role in Disney's 2015 live-action remake of Cinderella?
questions.explanation: Lily James stars as Cinderella in this live-action version, directed by Kenneth Branagh.

questions.question: In the live-action version of The Jungle Book, which actor voices Baloo the bear?
questions.explanation: Bill Murray provides the voice of the easygoing bear Baloo in the 2016 live-action remake.

questions.question: Who plays Belle in the 2017 live-action Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: Emma Watson stars as Belle in the live-action musical remake of Beauty and the Beast.

questions.question: Who voices the Genie in the 2019 live-action Aladdin?
questions.explanation: Will Smith takes on the role of the Genie in the live-action remake of Aladdin.

questions.question: The 2014 film Maleficent retells Sleeping Beauty from the perspective of which character?
questions.explanation: Maleficent reframes the classic Sleeping Beauty story from the viewpoint of the horned villainess herself.

questions.question: Who plays the title role in Cruella, depicting a famous villain's early life?
questions.explanation: Emma Stone plays young Estella as she transforms into the fashion villain Cruella de Vil.

questions.question: Which director known for gothic visuals helmed the 2019 live-action Dumbo?
questions.explanation: Tim Burton, known for his distinctive visual style, directed the 2019 live-action Dumbo remake.

questions.question: Which director helmed both the live-action Jungle Book and The Lion King remakes?
questions.explanation: Jon Favreau directed both the 2016 Jungle Book and the 2019 photorealistic Lion King remakes.

questions.question: In the 2016 live-action Pete's Dragon, what is the dragon's name?
questions.explanation: The furry green dragon who befriends the orphaned boy Pete is named Elliott in this remake.

questions.question: Who plays Lady Tremaine, the wicked stepmother, in the 2015 live-action Cinderella?
questions.explanation: Cate Blanchett plays the cold and calculating stepmother Lady Tremaine in this live-action remake.

questions.question: In The Absent-Minded Professor, what is the name of the bouncy substance Professor Brainard invents?
questions.explanation: Professor Brainard accidentally invents Flubber, a flying rubber substance with amazing bouncing properties.

questions.question: What is the name of the anthropomorphic Volkswagen Beetle race car in The Love Bug?
questions.explanation: Herbie is the spirited, self-aware Volkswagen Beetle who becomes a champion racer in this classic film.

questions.question: In Swiss Family Robinson, what do the shipwrecked family build to live in?
questions.explanation: The Robinson family constructs an elaborate multi-level treehouse after being shipwrecked on a deserted island.

questions.question: In Old Yeller, in which region does the Coates family live?
questions.explanation: Old Yeller is set on a frontier homestead in the Texas Hill Country in the years after the Civil War.

questions.question: In Pollyanna, what game does the cheerful heroine teach the townspeople to play?
questions.explanation: Pollyanna teaches the townspeople the Glad Game, finding something to be glad about in every situation.

questions.question: In the 1961 Parent Trap, Hayley Mills plays a pair of separated what?
questions.explanation: Hayley Mills plays twin sisters, separated as infants, who meet by chance years later at summer camp.

questions.question: In 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea, what is the name of Captain Nemo's submarine?
questions.explanation: Captain Nemo commands the Nautilus, an advanced submarine that explores the ocean depths.

questions.question: In the 2003 Freaky Friday, a mother and daughter swap bodies after eating what?
questions.explanation: Magical fortune cookies from a Chinese restaurant cause the mother and daughter to switch bodies overnight.

questions.question: In Enchanted, Amy Adams plays an animated princess transported to which real city?
questions.explanation: Princess Giselle is pushed through a portal from her animated kingdom into modern-day New York City.

questions.question: In The Sorcerer's Apprentice, Nicolas Cage's sorcerer trains his apprentice in which city?
questions.explanation: Balthazar Blake searches modern New York City for the Prime Merlinean destined to defeat Morgana.

questions.question: Saving Mr. Banks depicts Walt Disney's efforts to secure film rights to which classic book?
questions.explanation: Saving Mr. Banks dramatizes Walt Disney's long negotiation with author P.L. Travers over the film rights to Mary Poppins.

questions.question: Remember the Titans is based on the true story of an integrated football team in which state?
questions.explanation: Remember the Titans follows a newly integrated Virginia high school football team in the early 1970s.

questions.question: Miracle tells the true story of which team's improbable Olympic victory?
questions.explanation: Miracle recounts the 1980 U.S. Olympic hockey team's stunning upset victory over the Soviet team.

questions.question: In Queen of Katwe, the young protagonist rises to prominence playing which game?
questions.explanation: Queen of Katwe tells the true story of a Ugandan girl who becomes a formidable chess champion.

questions.question: What is the name of Hector Barbossa's pet monkey in the Pirates of the Caribbean films?
questions.explanation: Barbossa's mischievous capuchin monkey, also cursed alongside the crew, is named Jack.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

questions.question: Who serves as Jack Sparrow's loyal first mate throughout much of the Pirates series?
questions.explanation: Joshamee Gibbs is Jack Sparrow's superstitious and dependable first mate across the film series.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

questions.question: What is the name of Ben Gates's skeptical father in the National Treasure films?
questions.explanation: Patrick Gates initially doubts the family legend but eventually helps his son Ben find the treasure.

questions.question: In Curse of the Black Pearl, what happens to Barbossa's cursed crew in moonlight?
questions.explanation: The Aztec gold curse reveals itself in moonlight, when Barbossa's crew appears as living skeletons.
questions.sourceTitle: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

questions.question: In Mary Poppins Returns, what is the Banks family in danger of losing to the bank?
questions.explanation: Michael Banks risks losing the family home at Cherry Tree Lane over an unpaid bank loan.

questions.question: Which iconic castle sits at the center of Magic Kingdom at Walt Disney World?
questions.explanation: Cinderella Castle is the centerpiece of Magic Kingdom, standing at the heart of the park.

questions.question: Which castle serves as the icon of Disneyland Park in California?
questions.explanation: Sleeping Beauty Castle has stood at the heart of Disneyland Park since it opened in 1955.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom attraction sends riders on an indoor roller coaster through outer space?
questions.explanation: Space Mountain is a high-speed indoor coaster located in Tomorrowland at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom land is home to Space Mountain and the PeopleMover?
questions.explanation: Tomorrowland at Magic Kingdom features futuristic attractions like Space Mountain.

questions.question: Which boat ride features hundreds of singing dolls representing cultures around the world?
questions.explanation: it's a small world is a boat ride featuring hundreds of singing dolls celebrating world cultures.

questions.question: Which attraction features skippers narrating a comedic voyage through rivers of the world?
questions.explanation: Jungle Cruise features skippers guiding boats past animatronic animals with scripted comedic narration.

questions.question: Which Magic Kingdom land is themed to a spooky Southern mansion filled with 999 ghosts?
questions.explanation: The Haunted Mansion is located in Liberty Square, themed after colonial America.

questions.question: Which pirate-themed boat ride inspired a popular movie franchise starring Johnny Depp?
questions.explanation: Pirates of the Caribbean, a classic dark boat ride, inspired the blockbuster film series.

questions.question: EPCOT's giant geodesic sphere icon is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Spaceship Earth is the massive geodesic sphere that serves as EPCOT's iconic entrance attraction.

questions.question: Which section of EPCOT features pavilions themed to countries from around the world?
questions.explanation: World Showcase surrounds a lagoon at EPCOT with pavilions representing eleven different countries.

questions.question: Which country pavilion at EPCOT's World Showcase features a replica of the Eiffel Tower?
questions.explanation: The France pavilion at EPCOT's World Showcase includes a scaled-down model of the Eiffel Tower.

questions.question: Which EPCOT pavilion is home to the attraction Soarin' Around the World?
questions.explanation: Soarin' Around the World is a hang-gliding simulator ride located in The Land pavilion at EPCOT.

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios attraction drops riders in a runaway elevator inside a haunted hotel?
answers: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror
questions.explanation: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror simulates a haunted elevator plunging at the fictional Hollywood Tower Hotel.
questions.sourceTitle: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror

questions.question: Which Hollywood Studios coaster launches riders through loops set to rock music?
questions.explanation: Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring Aerosmith launches riders through loops set to rock music.

questions.question: Which land at Disney's Hollywood Studios is themed after a galaxy far, far away?
questions.explanation: Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge is themed to the planet Batuu, set in the Star Wars universe.

questions.question: Which giant artificial tree serves as the icon of Disney's Animal Kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Tree of Life stands at the center of Animal Kingdom and is carved with hundreds of animal figures.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom attraction takes guests on a truck ride through African savannas?
questions.explanation: Kilimanjaro Safaris takes guests on an open-truck ride through a recreated African savanna.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom coaster is themed around a legendary snow creature in the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: Expedition Everest features a mountain coaster themed around the legend of the Yeti.

questions.question: Which Animal Kingdom land is themed after the movie Avatar?
questions.explanation: Pandora - The World of Avatar features bioluminescent plants and floating mountains inspired by the film.

questions.question: Which Disney California Adventure land is themed around the Pixar film Cars?
questions.explanation: Cars Land recreates Radiator Springs and features the attraction Radiator Springs Racers.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Park mountain attraction sends bobsleds racing around a snowy peak?
questions.explanation: The Matterhorn Bobsleds attraction sends sleds racing through a scale model of the Swiss mountain.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Park attraction was the world's first tubular steel roller coaster?
questions.explanation: The Matterhorn Bobsleds, opened in 1959, was the world's first tubular steel roller coaster track.

questions.question: Which park at the Tokyo Disney Resort features Cinderella Castle at its center?
questions.explanation: Tokyo Disneyland, which opened in 1983, features Cinderella Castle at the center of the park.

questions.question: Which Disney park is themed entirely around nautical exploration and features a giant volcano?
questions.explanation: Tokyo DisneySea is built around a large volcano called Mount Prometheus and various nautical ports.

questions.question: In which country is Disneyland Paris located?
questions.explanation: Disneyland Paris is located in Marne-la-Vallee, a suburb east of Paris, France.

questions.question: Which Disney park opened in 2005 in the Chinese special administrative region of Hong Kong?
questions.explanation: Hong Kong Disneyland opened in 2005, becoming the second Disney theme park resort in Asia.

questions.question: Which Disney park, opened in 2016 near Shanghai, is the first Disney park in mainland China?
questions.explanation: Shanghai Disneyland opened in 2016, becoming the first and only Disney theme park in mainland China.

questions.question: Which mode of transportation glides above Walt Disney World connecting several resorts and parks?
questions.explanation: The Disney Skyliner is a gondola lift system connecting Hollywood Studios, EPCOT, and nearby resorts.

questions.question: Which elevated train system connects Magic Kingdom to the Transportation and Ticket Center?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Monorail system links Magic Kingdom with the Transportation and Ticket Center and resorts.

questions.question: Which Tomorrowland ride glides guests slowly above the land on an elevated track?

questions.explanation: Disney's Animal Kingdom Lodge features savanna views where guests can spot live African animals.

questions.question: Which Disneyland Resort hotel connects directly into Disney California Adventure via a walkway?
questions.explanation: Disney's Grand Californian Hotel & Spa has a direct entrance connecting into Disney California Adventure.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort hotel is themed to the islands of the South Pacific?
questions.explanation: Disney's Polynesian Village Resort is themed to the islands of the South Pacific.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resorts are themed to a nautical New England harbor town from the 1880s?
answers: Disney's Yacht Club and Beach Club Resorts
questions.explanation: Disney's Yacht Club and Beach Club Resorts are themed to a nautical New England harbor from the 1880s.

questions.question: What is the original flagship hotel of the Disneyland Resort, opened in 1955?
questions.explanation: The Disneyland Hotel opened in 1955 as the original resort hotel serving Disneyland Park guests.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort recreates a lively French Quarter and Louisiana bayou setting?
questions.explanation: Disney's Port Orleans Resort is themed to New Orleans' French Quarter and the Louisiana bayou.

questions.question: What type of transportation connects Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, and several Walt Disney World resorts overhead?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney World Monorail system links Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, and nearby resort hotels.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World transportation system uses gondola cabins suspended from cables?
questions.explanation: The Disney Skyliner is an aerial gondola system connecting Hollywood Studios, EPCOT, and several resorts.

questions.question: Besides the monorail, which mode of transportation carries guests across the Seven Seas Lagoon to Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: A ferryboat carries guests across the Seven Seas Lagoon from the Transportation and Ticket Center to Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: What is the name of the slow-moving people-mover attraction in Magic Kingdom's Tomorrowland?
questions.explanation: The Tomorrowland Transit Authority PeopleMover carries guests on a leisurely tour above Tomorrowland.

questions.question: Which vintage-style transportation vehicles carry guests along Main Street, U.S.A.?
questions.explanation: Double-decker omnibuses are among the vintage vehicles that carry guests along Main Street, U.S.A.

questions.question: What type of boats shuttle guests between EPCOT's World Showcase and nearby resort hotels?
questions.explanation: Friendship boats carry guests across World Showcase Lagoon between EPCOT and nearby resorts.

questions.question: Which railroad circles the perimeter of Disneyland Park, stopping at several themed lands?
questions.explanation: The Disneyland Railroad circles the park's perimeter, stopping at several themed lands.

questions.question: In what year did Disney's Magical Express airport shuttle service end?
questions.explanation: Disney's Magical Express provided complimentary transportation between Orlando International Airport and Disney resorts until it ended in January 2022.

questions.question: Which Skyliner station provides direct gondola access to Disney's Hollywood Studios?
questions.explanation: The Disney Skyliner includes a dedicated station providing direct access to Disney's Hollywood Studios.

questions.question: Fantasmic! is a long-running nighttime show performed at Disneyland Park and which other Disney park?
questions.explanation: Fantasmic! is a nighttime show performed at both Disneyland Park and Disney's Hollywood Studios.

questions.question: Festival of the Lion King is a live stage show featuring puppetry and acrobatics at which park?
questions.explanation: Festival of the Lion King is a live stage show combining puppetry, acrobatics, and music at Disney's Animal Kingdom.

questions.question: Which retired EPCOT nighttime show combined fireworks and lasers around World Showcase Lagoon?
questions.explanation: Illuminations: Reflections of Earth was a long-running fireworks and laser show performed on World Showcase Lagoon.

questions.question: The Main Street Electrical Parade first debuted in 1972 at which Disney park?
questions.explanation: The Main Street Electrical Parade debuted at Disneyland Park in 1972, lit by thousands of small bulbs.

questions.question: Which retired Magic Kingdom nighttime parade eventually succeeded the Main Street Electrical Parade there?
questions.explanation: SpectroMagic was a retired nighttime parade that succeeded the Main Street Electrical Parade at Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: The Festival of Fantasy Parade at Magic Kingdom features a large fire-breathing float themed to which villain?
questions.explanation: The Festival of Fantasy Parade includes a towering Maleficent dragon float that breathes fire.

questions.question: Which all-LED nighttime parade debuted at Disneyland Park during its Diamond Celebration in 2015?
questions.explanation: Paint the Night was an all-LED parade that debuted at Disneyland Park for its 60th anniversary celebration.

questions.question: Voices of Liberty, an a cappella singing group, regularly performs in which EPCOT pavilion?
questions.explanation: Voices of Liberty is an a cappella group that performs patriotic songs in EPCOT's American Adventure pavilion.

questions.question: Jedi Training is an interactive stage show found in Star Wars-themed lands, where guests do what?
questions.explanation: Jedi Training is a stage show where young guests learn to duel with lightsabers against Star Wars villains.

questions.question: In what year did Walt Disney World's Magic Kingdom officially open to the public?
questions.explanation: Magic Kingdom opened on October 1, 1971, as the first park at Walt Disney World.

questions.question: In what year did the original Disneyland Park open in Anaheim, California?
